name: Discord

rateLimits.global: 50 requests per second (default), up to 1,200/s for qualified bots
rateLimits.perRoute: Varies by endpoint, indicated via X-RateLimit-Bucket header
rateLimits.notes: Rate limits are per-route and global. Parse X-RateLimit-* response headers. Do not hardcode limits.

scim.notes: Discord does not support SCIM provisioning. User management is done via bot API endpoints for guild members.

sdk.notes: discord.js is the most popular Node.js library for the Discord API. Additional packages: @discordjs/rest for REST-only usage, @discordjs/core for minimal usage.

webhooks.registrationEndpoint: Gateway connection (WebSocket) or Interaction endpoint URL in app settings
webhooks.notes: Discord uses a WebSocket Gateway for real-time events, not HTTP webhooks. GUILD_MEMBERS is a privileged intent requiring approval for bots in 100+ guilds. Discord 'webhooks' are for sending messages to channels, not receiving events.

sandbox.notes: No official developer sandbox. Developers create free test servers (guilds) for development. No cost to create servers or bot applications. No access to premium features without Nitro/boosts.

needs_verification: Whether addGuildMember (PUT) requires the user to have authorized with guilds.join scope
Exact rate limits per route for member management endpoints
Whether GUILD_MEMBERS privileged intent auto-approval threshold is still 100 guilds
